data.title: Bitcoin set to rise on weekly basis, stays below $100K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is on course to rise 5% on a weekly basis, but stayed below the $100K mark.

The world's largest digital currency, which saw a robust performance in 2024, is expected to see another strong year.

However, the cryptocurrency seems to have had a rocky start. BTC dropped to $91.3K on Monday, days after Janet Yellen, secretary of the U.S. Treasury, warned Congress that the government is likely to reach its limit on borrowing as early as Jan. 14.

Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price doesn't appear likely to recover soon, according to its history in relation to debt ceiling episodes, according to a media report.

According to a CoinDesk report, increasing the debt ceiling has historically led to Bitcoin dropping or underperforming in the past five instances.

In the week and until the time of writing, BTC stayed below the $100K threshold, and reached as high as $98.8K.

On Thursday, BlackRock’s IBIT, also recorded outflows of $332.6M, making it its largest ever, according to data from SoSoValue. The Nasdaq-listed IBIT is also the largest public fund tracking Bitcoin (BTC-USD).

"I advise against aggressively going long on Bitcoin at current prices; waiting for lower sentiment and prices may offer better buying opportunities," SA contributor Oriental Trader said.

The history of Bitcoin suggests that if an investor waits, the price will fall back into a lull at some point and when sentiment is low and negative, then it could be an interesting time to purchase, Oriental Trader added.

SA contributor, Frank Holmes, also advocated the need to have Bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the 60/40 portfolio.

"Despite its volatility, Bitcoin's market cap and growth potential make it a compelling addition alongside gold for wealth preservation and growth," Holmes said.

"Another sign of BTC heading towards being a mainstream asset class is its declining realized volatility, which would eventually add more diversification benefits and alpha to the traditional 60/40 portfolio. Volatility should continue to decline as an asset class matures, as our long-standing view is that crypto would be no different," said Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SOFA.

More Notable News

Digihost Technology (DGHI) produced around 35 BTC in December, compared to 37 produced in the previous month.

Bitfarms (BITF) reported a month on month growth in the total BTC earned in December.

Do Kwon, the former CEO of Terraform Labs, which collapsed in 2022, was handed over on Tuesday to U.S. authorities by Montenegro, where he was arrested in March 2023.

Crypto-linked stocks were higher in premarket trade on Thursday, as traders hope 2025 to be a robust year for cryptocurrencies.

Bit Digital (BTBT) executed a master service agreement providing for 576 Nvidia H200 GPUs over a two-year term to a new client, an AI Compute Fund managed by DNA Holdings Venture, the company said.

MicroStrategy (MSTR) acquired 2,138 bitcoins (BTC-USD) for $209M in the past week, bringing its holdings to 446,400 bitcoins as of Dec. 29, 2024, which were bought for ~$27.9B, the company disclosed on Monday.

Bitcoin, Ether moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was up 1.3% to $98.8K on Friday as at 2:29 pm. Ether (ETH-USD) rose 4.3% to $3.6K.

data.title: Honda Motor reports annual results
data.description:
• Honda Motor's (NYSE:HMC) American Honda reported annual sales of 1.42M Honda & Acura vehicles in 2024, an increase of 8.8% YoY.
  • Honda brand sales of 1.29M units, up 11.1% YoY.
  • Annual SUV sales of over 760,000 units.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Toyota Motor North America reports year-end results
data.description:
• Toyota Motor North America (OTC:TMNA) reported year-end 2024 U.S. sales of 2,332,623 vehicles, an increase of 3.7% on a volume basis and an increase of 3.1% on a daily selling rate basis compared to 2023.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Microsoft says it will spend $80B in data centers in 2025
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) said in a blog post on Friday that it will spend $80B in data centers this year.
  • Microsoft shares rose 1.3% in midday trading following the post.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Subaru of America reports December sales up 7.1%
data.description:
• Subaru of America (OTCPK:FUJHY) on Friday, reported 29 consecutive months of increased sales with 61,871 vehicles sold in December 2024, a 7.1 percent increase compared with December 2023.
  • Subaru ended calendar year 2024 with 667,725 vehicle sales, a 5.6 percent increase compared with calendar year 2023.
  • For calendar year 2024, Crosstrek was the top performer by volume, achieving its best year ever with 181,811 vehicle sales (+14 percent).
  • In its second full year of sales, the Solterra EV continued to grow with its best December ever and best year ever, resulting in 12,447 vehicle sales (+40 percent).

data.title: U.S. oil rig count down 1, gas rigs up 1 - Baker Hughes
data.description:
The number of active rigs drilling for oil and gas in the U.S. remained unchanged for the third straight week at 589, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest report.

Drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. fell by 1 to 482, while rigs directed at natural gas gained 1 to 103 and 4 rigs were classified as miscellaneous.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin stayed flat at 301, the Eagle Ford count fell by 1 to 38, and the Williston Basin added 1 to 36.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: NeOnc files for direct listing in wake of pulled IPO
data.description:
Brain cancer drug developer NeOnc Technologies (NASDAQ:NTHI) plans to go public via a direct listing of up to 2.1M of its common shares.

The company will not receive proceeds from the offering, as the shares will be sold by registered stockholders. NeoOnc (NASDAQ:NTHI) hopes to list its shares on Nasdaq under the symbol NTHI. RBW Capital Partners is serving as financial advisor, according to an SEC filing.

NeOnc (NTHI) didn't disclose a reference price for the stock, but said it has private placement agreements to sell 625K of its common shares for $16 per share prior to the listing.

The company filed for a traditional IPO in December 2023, seeking around $50M, but withdrew the filing in June 2024.

Based in Westlake Village, Calif., NeOnc's (NTHI) lead drug candidate is NEO100, a purified form of perillyl alcohol which is delivered intranasally. The product is currently in Phase 2 testing for the treatment of malignant glioma and meningioma.

data.title: Acelyrin spikes on upcoming mid-stage data update on eye disease drug
data.description:
Acelyrin (NASDAQ:SLRN) shares traded higher on Friday after the company scheduled an investor meeting for next week to discuss updated data from a Phase 2 trial for lonigutamab, an injectable targeted at thyroid eye disease (TED).

At the virtual event, the company will also disclose a design for its Phase 3 LONGITUDE program for the drug, developed using FDA feedback received at a recent end-of-Phase 2 meeting, the Agoura Hills, California-based biotech said in a press release late Thursday.

The company added that Acelyrin (NASDAQ:SLRN) CEO Mina Kim, medical chief Shep Mpofu, and commercial chief Ken Loc will attend the event scheduled for Jan. 6 at 4:30 PM ET.

Drs. Andrea Kossler and Prem Subramanian, members of the biotech’s scientific and patient advisory group and academics of the Stanford University School of Medicine and University of Colorado School of Medicine, respectively, will also join the event.

data.title: Evercore ISI predicts Tech will lead the S&P 500 to another 20% upside year in 2025
data.description:
Evercore ISI expressed on Friday that Wall Street will likely see more of what it experienced in 2024 as the investment institution stated “Fill IT up again” in 2025 indicating its positive view on “IT.”

Evercore ISI echoed that “the bull market in the S&P remains strong” and is intact as the firm speculates “another 22% upside to 7,200 in 2025.” For reference, as 2025 is now just underway, the benchmark index sits at 5,940.

Supporting its view of a strong market is the Info Tech sector of the economy, as Evercore ISI believes tech will lead markets higher once again this year. Furthermore, from a sub-industry perspective both semiconductors and software were highlighted.

Regarding the semiconductor space, the investment bank added “semis are coiled for a major move higher,” and stressed its positions in high conviction buys: Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM).

In terms of the software industry, the firm said “Software will outperform as IT did in ’24” and highlighted shares of Microsoft (MSFT).

As for the magnificent seven names: (AAPL), (AMZN), (GOOG), (GOOGL), (META), (MSFT), (NVDA), and (TSLA), the financial firm stated: “Buy them all.”

data.title: 2024 recap: Small caps have now lagged every year since 2017 – BofA
data.description:
Small caps (NYSEARCA:IWM) had the worst upside in 2024.

The Russell 2000 (IWM) was up 11.5% on a total return basis. The Russell MidCap (IWR), on the other hand, was up 15.3%, and the Russell 1000 (IWB) was up 24.5%.

“Small caps (IWM) have now lagged large in every calendar year since 2017, with 2024’s 13 percentage points of underperformance the largest of any year since 1998,” said Savita Subramanian, head of U.S. Equity Strategy and U.S. Quantitative Strategy at BofA, in a note.

Technology (PSCT) was the best-performing small cap sector, up 25%; and energy (PSCE) was the worst-performing, down 3%.

Also, in December, small caps (IWM) sold off 8.3%, particularly after the Federal Reserve meeting, with all sectors down, underperforming mid-caps -7.0%, and large-caps -2.8%, said Subramanian.

“We prefer mid-caps (IWR) over small, and mega in 2025.”
